reasoning: The function creates a TCP socket bound to localhost on an ephemeral port (port 0). The operating system assigns an available port, which is stored in self.sql_proxy_tcp_port. The socket is kept in self.reserved_tcp_socket to keep the port reserved until it is closed or released. The code does not close the socket, so the port remains allocated.
output: This method reserves a free local TCP port by binding a socket to 127.0.0.1 on port 0. The OS assigns an available ephemeral port, which is stored in self.sql_proxy_tcp_port. The socket is kept in self.reserved_tcp_socket to hold onto the port for later use. Note: you should close the reserved socket when the port is no longer needed to release the OS resource, or provide a cleanup method to do so. If you intend to reuse the port immediately, ensure you manage the lifecycle of self.reserved_tcp_socket accordingly (e.g., close it when done or upgrade to a listening socket as needed).
